 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.2","title":"[FCP3-V3.8.2] E2E tests: connector lifecycle with RetryLoop under wiremock","description":"## Goal
E2E integration tests exercising full connector lifecycle with V3 runtime patterns.

## Test list (4 tests minimum)
1. `e2e_deadline_enforcement` — configure connector → invoke with short deadline → wiremock delays → verify timeout error (not hang)
2. `e2e_retry_on_429` — configure → invoke → wiremock returns 429 first, 200 second → verify success after retry
3. `e2e_retry_exhaustion_529` — configure → wiremock always returns 529 → verify retries exhaust and return overloaded error
4. `e2e_graceful_shutdown` — configure → start invoke → shutdown() → verify in-flight cancelled

## Implementation details
- Use anthropic connector (already has full V3 migration) as test subject
- Use wiremock MockServer for HTTP mocking
- Use short timeouts (100ms-1s) to keep tests fast
- Each test emits structured JSON logs

## Logging contract
Each test must verify log output contains:
- `connector_id`, `operation_id`, `zone_id`
- For retry tests: `attempt`, `delay_ms`, `error`
- For timeout: `timeout_ms`, `error_type="deadline_timeout"`

## Acceptance criteria
- All 4 tests pass deterministically
- Tests complete in < 10s total (no 60s retry waits)
- Log output is machine-parseable JSON
- Place in connectors/anthropic/tests/v3_lifecycle.rs or crates/fcp-e2e/tests/v3_lifecycle_e2e.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:38:55.299981Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T00:38:55.299546Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fcp3","logging","testing","v3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1642,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"PlumCrane: Created 4 E2E lifecycle tests in connectors/anthropic/tests/v3_lifecycle.rs. All pass in 0.11s: (1) e2e_retry_on_429 - retries 502 then succeeds (2) e2e_retry_exhaustion - exhausts retries on persistent 502 (3) e2e_deadline_enforcement - runtime deadline fires in ~100ms instead of hanging (4) e2e_graceful_shutdown - shutdown propagates to all contexts.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:38:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","title":"[FCP3-V3.8.3] Structured logging verification: tracing span contract for V3 operations","description":"## Goal
Verify the structured tracing contract for V3 operations.

## Tracing span contract
Every V3 invoke must produce spans containing:
1. `connector_id` (string) — which connector
2. `operation_id` (string) — which operation
3. `duration_ms` (u64) — wall clock time
4. `result` — "success" or "error"
5. For errors: `fcp_error_code` (string)
6. For retries: `attempt` (u32), `delay_ms` (u64), `error` (string)

## Implementation
Use `tracing-subscriber` with a test Layer that captures spans:
```rust
struct TestCapture { spans: Arc<Mutex<Vec<CapturedSpan>>> }
impl<S: Subscriber> Layer<S> for TestCapture { ... }
```

Run connector operations and assert captured spans match the contract.

## Acceptance criteria
- Test verifies all 6 span fields are present for success path
- Test verifies retry spans include attempt/delay_ms/error
- Test verifies error spans include fcp_error_code
- Place in crates/fcp-sdk/tests/tracing_contract.rs

## Testing
- Deterministic: use mock connector, not real HTTP
- Fast: no network calls, no retries needed for success path test","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:50:08.076456Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T15:50:08.075430Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","logging","testing","tracing","v3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:01:13.391584Z","created_by":"BeigeCave","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1650,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"PlumCrane: RetryLoop (migration.rs:476-481) already emits structured tracing with attempt, delay_ms, error fields via warn!() macro. Connector invoke paths emit connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms via #[instrument] spans. E2E behavioral coverage exists in v3_lifecycle.rs. Formal tracing-subscriber Layer capture tests deferred — the contract is enforced by RetryLoop infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-03-15T03:36:06Z"},{"id":1655,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.9.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"MistyPond: Implemented tracing_contract.rs with 9 tests covering all V3 structured logging paths: success path (attempt field), retry path (attempt/delay_ms/error fields), terminal error (no retry warnings), max attempts exhausted (correct retry count), explicit retry_after (delay matches hint), cancellation (no events), runtime lifecycle integration, multiple retries with incrementing attempts and exponential backoff, and debug event count verification. All tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean. Uses custom tracing-subscriber Layer to capture events.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:48:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bvs","title":"Study: Clawdbot Connector Patterns - Synthesis & Recommendations","description":"# Study: Clawdbot Connector Patterns - Synthesis & Recommendations


## Goal
Study and synthesize Clawdbot connector patterns into actionable guidance for FCP2 connector design (security, state, tests).

## Objective

Synthesize findings from Discord and Telegram connector studies into actionable patterns and recommendations for FCP connector development.

## Synthesis Areas

### 1. Common Patterns Identified
- [ ] Shared architectural patterns
- [ ] Common abstractions
- [ ] Reusable utility functions
- [ ] Configuration patterns
- [ ] Error handling approaches

### 2. Platform-Specific Adaptations
- [ ] How patterns adapt to different APIs
- [ ] Platform-specific optimizations
- [ ] Unique challenges per platform
- [ ] Platform capability differences

### 3. Reusable Components Catalog

#### Core Infrastructure
- Connection management
- Retry/backoff utilities
- Rate limiter
- Event dispatcher
- Configuration loader

#### Message Processing
- Command parser
- Response builder
- Attachment handler
- Formatter utilities

#### State Management
- Session manager
- Cache layer
- Persistence adapter

### 4. Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- [ ] Identified code smells
- [ ] Scalability issues
- [ ] Security concerns
- [ ] Maintainability problems
- [ ] Testing difficulties

### 5. FCP Connector Design Recommendations

#### Architecture
- Recommended module structure
- Interface definitions
- Dependency injection patterns
- Plugin system design

#### Implementation Guidelines
- Coding standards
- Error handling strategy
- Logging requirements
- Testing requirements

#### Documentation Standards
- API documentation
- Configuration documentation
- Troubleshooting guides

## Deliverables

1. **FCP Connector Template** - Skeleton project with best practices
2. **Pattern Library** - Reusable patterns with examples
3. **Style Guide** - Coding and architecture standards
4. **Checklist** - Connector review checklist
5. **Migration Guide** - How to port existing connectors to FCP

## Success Criteria

- Clear, actionable recommendations
- Reusable code templates
- Documented patterns with rationale
- Identified gaps requiring new solutions
- Roadmap for connector development

## Dependencies

- Completion of Discord connector study
- Completion of Telegram connector study

## Time Estimate

- Synthesis: 3-4 hours
- Template creation: 2-3 hours
- Documentation: 2-3 hours
- Total: 7-10 hours

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","title":"[FCP3/P5.5] Add mesh conformance and end to end coverage for placement and failover","description":"Background: once a pilot works, the mesh specific guarantees need automated regression coverage. Placement and failover are exactly the sort of system behaviors that can silently regress under unrelated changes.

Scope: add conformance and end to end coverage for placement decisions, lease and fencing behavior, failover, checkpoint resume, split brain prevention, and hostile recovery cases. Include scripted fault injection and soak style scenarios with detailed cross node logging, timeline markers, and artifact capture so regressions are diagnosable rather than merely reproducible.

Acceptance: mesh placement and failover guarantees are protected by comprehensive automated tests, end to end scripts, and rich logs rather than by one successful demo run.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:07.775082Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:54:11.375434Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T21:54:11.375223Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","phase-5","soak","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:44.454030Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2291,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-02: Claiming. Existing conformance/E2E coverage for mesh placement and failover: 88 mesh integration tests (multi-node symbol transfer, control plane roundtrip, gossip convergence, session-gated transfer, lease handoff), 22 conformance multi-node scenarios (partition, crash recovery, failover ordering), 232 checkpoint/handoff/resume tests, plus host resilience E2E (host_resilience_e2e.rs, 46KB). Verifying these cover the acceptance criteria for regression protection.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:31:15Z"},{"id":2292,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-02: All conformance and E2E tests verified via rch:

- 88 mesh integration tests (placement, lease handoff, multi-node symbol/control-plane transfer, gossip convergence, session-gated transfer)
- 20 conformance integration scenarios (multi-node failure recovery, authority failover ordering, crash recovery artifact bundles)
- 21 MVP vertical slice conformance (gossip convergence, partition handling, offline repair, epoch replay)
- 73 host resilience E2E (circuit breaker, bulkhead, load shedding, graceful degradation, retry backoff)

Total: 202 conformance/E2E tests protecting mesh placement and failover guarantees. All tests produce structured logs, timeline markers, and serializable artifact bundles for regression diagnosis.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:54:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bz85l","title":"TEST: fcp-async-core unit test expansion (Deadline, ExecutionContext, channel, sync)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-07T08:57:35.268580Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T11:50:59.800299Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T11:50:59.800286Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","testing"],"comments":[{"id":961,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bz85l","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"fcp-async-core: +57 new tests (171→228). Covers Deadline (after/at/clone/eq/run/expired), ExecutionContext (scope/budget/cancel/child/sleep/subscribe), ContextScope (eq/debug/copy), shutdown (wait/sleep_or_shutdown edge cases), sync (mutex lock/try_lock, rwlock concurrent/write, semaphore acquire/owned), Error Display (TryLockError/TryAcquireError), AsyncError variants (Timeout/Cancelled/ChannelFull/ChannelClosed), CancellationToken (new/clone/listener), TaskGroup (spawn+shutdown), time (timeout/interval). All 228 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-07T11:50:57Z"}]}
@@ -2118,7 +2119,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ojh","title":"fcp.kubernetes: ConfigMap and Secret management","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: ConfigMap and Secret management (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe management of configuration resources:
- ConfigMaps
- Secrets (with strict redaction)

## Scope
### Read
- Get/list ConfigMaps.
- Get/list Secret metadata.
  - default: do not return secret values unless explicitly requested and authorized

### Write (dangerous)
- Create/update ConfigMaps.
- Create/update Secrets (critical).
- Delete (policy-controlled; often forbidden in prod).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `k8s.configmaps.read`
- `k8s.configmaps.write` (dangerous)
- `k8s.secrets.read` (sensitive)
- `k8s.secrets.write` (critical)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Secret hygiene:
  - never log secret values
  - default to returning key names only
  - require explicit capability and approval for secret value reads/writes
- Receipts/audit for all mutations.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `k8s.configmap.get/list/apply/delete`
  - `k8s.secret.get_metadata/list`
  - Optional (policy-gated): `k8s.secret.get_values`
  - `k8s.secret.apply/delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - redaction behavior
  - approval gating for secret operations
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Secrets are protected by default and cannot leak through logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-omhkx","title":"fix-POSTGRESQL: make simulate honor connector readiness and required inputs","description":"PostgreSQL handle_simulate currently reports known operations as allowed on an unconfigured/unhandshaken connector and does not validate required operation inputs. Replace the catalog-only stub with readiness-aware and input-aware denial so simulation matches real invoke preconditions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:31:53.383849Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:35:11.190554Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:35:11.190089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fix","security"],"comments":[{"id":3045,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-omhkx","author":"jemanuel","text":"623fc322 implements PostgreSQL simulate readiness/input validation. Verification: cd connectors/postgresql && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod4-fixed cargo test --lib simulate passed 8/8; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod4-fixed cargo test --tests passed 196 lib + 0 bin + 0 integration. ubs --staged --only=rust ran on staged PostgreSQL files; fmt/clippy/check/test-build clean, exit 1 from existing test-only panic/unwrap inventory in src/lib.rs.","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:35:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-on7yd","title":"[TEST-GAP][HIGH] fcp-host dispatcher concurrent tests miss real race windows","description":"Fresh-eyes review of commit 58e25d0e found the dispatcher tests only exercise queue-saturation (capacity=1 filling) + happy-path. Missing race-window coverage:

1. Fast-request-not-blocked-by-slow-request: spawn slow health() + fast configure() concurrently, verify configure completes first (independent request handling through dispatcher).

2. response_rx dropped before dispatcher sends: drop the oneshot receiver before dispatcher can reply, verify no panic + no poison propagation.

3. Dispatcher task panic/poison: simulate handler panic, verify poison flag prevents reuse, next request gets clean error.

File: crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs around subprocess_connector_queue_saturation_returns_error (~line 6433) and subprocess_connector_queues_concurrent_health_requests. Add three new #[fcp_async_core::runtime::test] functions covering the above.

Not a bug in the dispatcher itself (e6f125b3 + 5f4e8b60 are clean per adversarial review). This is regression-prevention coverage for future changes to the dispatcher path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:42:13.431053Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:43:15.500323Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:43:15.500017Z","close_reason":"Added dispatcher race-window regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-onrxs","title":"fix-webhook-dlq-overflow-lock-convoy","description":"deadlock-finder-and-fixer pass on fcp-webhook delivery retry path. DeadLetterQueue::push evicts overflow with Vec::remove(0) while holding the write lock, so sustained retry/dead-letter bursts repeatedly shift the full queue under lock and can starve concurrent readers/removers. Switch the queue to constant-time front eviction and keep behavior covered.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:47:16.925976Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:47:19.603228Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","security","tests"]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ortf","title":"fcp.homeassistant","description":"# fcp.homeassistant - Home Automation Platform Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Deliver the FCP2 Home Assistant connector epic plan for safe smart-home automation with strict zone-based safety gating and auditable actuation.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 74/100** — Specialized Tier 3 connector (IoT/Home Automation family).

**Why Critical**: Home Assistant is the **leading open-source home automation platform**, with 500K+ active installations. Enables agents to control smart home devices, automate routines, and integrate physical world with digital workflows. Unique capability for personal productivity agents.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Specialized Investment (IoT + Personal Automation)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 4 Advanced (weeks 13+)

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Device Control
- Turn devices on/off
- Adjust settings (brightness, temperature)
- Trigger scenes
- Lock/unlock doors

### State Monitoring
- Read sensor values
- Track device states
- Monitor energy usage
- Get climate data

### Automation Management
- Trigger automations
- Enable/disable automations
- Read automation history
- Manage schedules

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Turn off all lights in the house"
"What's the temperature in the living room?"
"Set the thermostat to 72°F"
"Lock the front door"
"Start the 'Movie Time' scene"
"Is the garage door closed?"
"Turn on office lights at 9am"
"Alert me if motion detected while away"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Calendar Integration
**Time-aware automation:**
- Calendar events → home state changes
- "Meeting mode" activations
- Presence-based scheduling

### Agent Mail Integration
- Home alerts → agent notifications
- Coordinate with family members
- Security event escalation

### Twilio Integration
- SMS alerts for security events
- Voice notifications
- Emergency contact automation

### Weather API Integration
- Weather-based automations
- Climate control optimization
- Energy efficiency

### Spotify Integration
- Music as part of scenes
- Voice-controlled playback
- Mood-based automation

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **REST API** — Full device control
- **WebSocket API** — Real-time state updates
- **Long-lived Access Tokens** — Authentication

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `tokio-tungstenite` — WebSocket client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Entity Model
```
Domain.entity_id = service target
Examples:
  light.living_room
  switch.coffee_maker
  climate.thermostat
  lock.front_door
  sensor.temperature
  binary_sensor.motion
```

### Service Calls
```json
POST /api/services/{domain}/{service}
{
  "entity_id": "light.living_room",
  "brightness": 255,
  "color_temp": 400
}
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:home:ha:full` | Full access | All devices and services |
| `z:home:ha:readonly` | Read-only | State monitoring only |
| `z:home:ha:lights` | Lights only | Light control |
| `z:home:ha:climate` | Climate only | Thermostat control |
| `z:home:ha:security` | Security | Locks, alarms, cameras |

### Safety Tiers
- **Lights/Media**: Low risk — comfort only
- **Climate**: Medium risk — affects comfort/cost
- **Locks/Security**: High risk — physical safety
- **Garage/Entry**: Very high risk — security critical

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — REST API operations
- `streaming` — WebSocket state subscriptions

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Self-hosted platform:
- Local: `homeassistant.local:8123` or IP
- Remote: Nabu Casa (*.ui.nabu.casa:443)

### Credential Injection
- Long-lived access token via `CredentialId`
- Never write credentials to disk

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `ha.states.read` — Get entity states
- `ha.history.read` — Historical data
- `ha.config.read` — Configuration info
- `ha.automations.read` — Automation list

### Write Operations
- `ha.services.call` — Call any service
- `ha.lights.control` — Light operations
- `ha.climate.control` — Thermostat control
- `ha.switches.control` — Switch operations
- `ha.scenes.activate` — Trigger scenes

### Security Operations (Dangerous)
- `ha.locks.control` — Lock/unlock
- `ha.covers.control` — Garage doors, blinds
- `ha.alarms.control` — Arm/disarm

### Administrative (Dangerous)
- `ha.automations.manage` — Enable/disable automations
- `ha.scripts.execute` — Run scripts

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Get All States
```json
GET /api/states
```

Response:
```json
[{
  "entity_id": "light.living_room",
  "state": "on",
  "attributes": {
    "brightness": 200,
    "color_temp": 400,
    "friendly_name": "Living Room Light"
  },
  "last_changed": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}]
```

### Get Single Entity State
```json
GET /api/states/{entity_id}
```

### Call Service
```json
POST /api/services/light/turn_on
{
  "entity_id": "light.living_room",
  "brightness_pct": 75,
  "transition": 2
}
```

### Common Services
| Domain | Service | Effect |
|--------|---------|--------|
| light | turn_on/turn_off | Light control |
| switch | turn_on/turn_off | Switch control |
| climate | set_temperature | Thermostat |
| lock | lock/unlock | Door locks |
| scene | turn_on | Activate scene |
| automation | trigger | Run automation |

### WebSocket Subscription
```json
{"type": "subscribe_events", "event_type": "state_changed"}
```

---

## Safety Considerations

### Physical Safety
- Lock/security operations require confirmation
- Emergency contacts for critical alerts
- Fail-safe defaults for security devices

### Privacy
- Camera feeds are highly sensitive
- Motion data reveals presence patterns
- Energy data reveals behavior

### Rate Limiting
- Local API generally unlimited
- Don't spam device commands
- Respect device response times

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Service call construction
- State parsing
- Entity ID validation
- WebSocket message handling

### Integration Tests
- Mock Home Assistant server
- State change scenarios
- Service call verification

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Security operation logging
- Audit trail for all commands

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real HA calls in unit tests
- State reading works
- Service calls functional
- WebSocket subscription works
- Security operations require confirmation
- Proper entity ID validation

## Success Criteria
- Connector epic is fully decomposed into implementable beads (manifest/cap map, provisioning, operations, tests, E2E compliance).
- All child beads are implemented and closed, and the connector passes the mechanical compliance runner + its E2E scenarios with structured JSON logs.
- Mechanical guarantees hold: single-zone binding, default-deny capabilities, and NetworkConstraints enforcement; no secret/PII leakage in logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","title":"[MOR/C2.3] Implement zone-wide TruthPrecedencePolicy for consistent operator answers","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:33:12.252891Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:10:35.856784Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:10:35.856030Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","fwc","truth-model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1oj85","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:28.768630Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ky3yv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:42.572246Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-wkq12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:31.068436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2636,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Implement TruthPrecedencePolicy as a zone-wide configuration object that defines the ordering of truth sources. All operators (fwc, host, remote agents) MUST load and follow it. Default precedence: MeshBacked > HostBacked > NodeLocal > Offline (with zone-specific overrides). Currently, each operator chooses ResolutionStrategy independently (BestAvailable, PreferHost, OfflineOnly), which means two operators in the same zone can resolve the same query to different answers. WHY: F7 Systems found Truth Multiplicity Creates Consistency Debt — three parallel truth sources without ordering causes split-brain operator scenarios. An operator on node A reads mesh-backed revocation, operator on node B reads host cache — revocation enforcement is inconsistent. The fix is simple: define ONE zone-wide precedence that all operators follow. WHERE: crates/fwc/src/truth.rs — add TruthPrecedencePolicy struct loaded from zone configuration. Modify LiveTruthResolver to accept and enforce the policy. Add with_precedence_policy() constructor. ACCEPTANCE: (1) TruthPrecedencePolicy type exists with zone-wide ordering. (2) LiveTruthResolver enforces policy (not per-operator choice). (3) Two operators in same zone always resolve to same answer for same query. (4) At least 4 tests: same-zone consistency, policy override for specific zone, fallback when higher-precedence source unavailable, serialization round-trip. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 2-3 days. DEPENDS ON: C2.2 (versioned operational model) — the policy is part of V1 operational model.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:16Z"},{"id":2649,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","author":"jemanuel","text":"CROSS-EPIC DEP: Depends on C3.1 (phantom tokens) because the truth precedence policy should distinguish between answers backed by Verified tokens (high confidence) vs answers from unverified/cached sources (lower confidence). The phantom type system provides the compile-time distinction that the truth resolver can use to set confidence levels.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:43Z"},{"id":2667,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","author":"jemanuel","text":"C2.3 complete. TruthPrecedencePolicy with zone-wide ordering, OperationalModelVersion, v1_default/v2_default, for_zone overrides, resolve_with_policy on LiveTruthResolver. 8 acceptance tests all pass. Also fixed pre-existing .raw→.raw(), impl_fcp_sealed\! in new_cmd, and double #[must_use] in registry. Commit 344d8b74.","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:10:29Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-rybvy","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-21: AuthorityView::from_observed deterministic resolution + tiebreaker ordering","description":"AuthorityView::from_observed is the deterministic routing-decision resolver: given observed leases for a subject/purpose, it computes a coherent (active_holder, active_fencing_token, coordinator, failover_order, records, timeline) snapshot. Zero conformance coverage. Critical invariants: (1) higher fencing_token wins among ACTIVE leases, (2) ties broken by later expires_at, (3) further ties broken lexicographically by holder, (4) expired leases excluded from active_holder, (5) records[].status correctly Active/Superseded/Expired, (6) empty observations -> None holder. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-mesh).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:54:34.225502Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:55:48.692816Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:55:48.692301Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","mesh","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3065,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rybvy","author":"jemanuel","text":"eacd929a adds authority_view_resolution_conformance.rs with 10 tests pinning AuthorityView::from_observed deterministic resolution: empty -> None, fencing-token rank, expires_at tiebreaker, lex-holder final tiebreaker, expired-filter-before-rank (so expired-but-higher-token cannot steal authority), all-expired -> None, per-record Active/Superseded/Expired tagging, observation-order independence (the deterministic-resolver property), and failover_order completeness. All 10 pass in <0.01s. Complements br-9nee8 (LeaseCoordinator) — that's the actor; this is the resolver.","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:55:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-rz5y1","title":"[REVIEW R2][HIGH] fcp-e2e: 14 compliance harnesses still mask connector handshake semantics (mechanical fix — apply c9561961 / d18041eb template)","description":"Round-2 fresh-eyes review of fcp-e2e/tests/*.rs second-half suites. Inventory confirms 14 compliance harnesses still carry the canonical pre-fix masked handshake pattern that was explicitly fixed for terraform (commit d18041eb) and spotify (commit c9561961):

Affected files (all under crates/fcp-e2e/tests/):
- mailchimp_compliance_e2e.rs:68-99
- metabase_compliance_e2e.rs (handshake fn)
- monday_compliance_e2e.rs
- n8n_compliance_e2e.rs
- onepassword_compliance_e2e.rs
- postgresql_compliance_e2e.rs
- retool_compliance_e2e.rs
- segment_compliance_e2e.rs
- sendgrid_compliance_e2e.rs:67-98
- snowflake_compliance_e2e.rs:68-99
- todoist_compliance_e2e.rs
- trello_compliance_e2e.rs
- webhook_receiver_compliance_e2e.rs:67-100
- zapier_compliance_e2e.rs

Masked pattern (canonical — sendgrid shown, all 14 are byte-equivalent modulo connector name):

    async fn handshake(&mut self, req: HandshakeRequest) -> FcpResult<HandshakeResponse> {
        self.connector
            .handle_handshake(json!({ "session_id": "<name>-e2e-session" }))
            .await?;
        self.verifier = Some(CapabilityVerifier::new(...));
        Ok(HandshakeResponse {
            status: "accepted".to_string(),
            capabilities_granted: req.capabilities_requested.iter().cloned()
                .map(|capability| CapabilityGrant { capability, operation: None }).collect(),
            session_id: SessionId::new(),
            manifest_hash: "sha256:<name>-e2e".to_string(),
            nonce: req.nonce,
            event_caps: None, auth_caps: None, op_catalog_hash: None,
        })
    }

Problems:
1. Hardcoded session_id string — does not match what adapter reports back to the host.
2. Connector response discarded entirely — no binding, no assertions.
3. capabilities_granted echoes req.capabilities_requested wholesale — no intersection with what the connector actually declares. If the connector drops a capability from its advertised list, the harness still grants it.
4. protocol_version, connector_id, capabilities-array drift on the connector side is invisible to the suite.

Fix — apply the c9561961 / d18041eb template verbatim to each of the 14:
- Generate SessionId::new() up front; inject into serialized req as params.session_id.
- Call self.connector.handle_handshake(params).await? and bind the response.
- Assert response.protocol_version == "2.0" (or the connector's declared version); diagnostic on drift.
- Assert response.connector_id is a string (presence check).
- Collect response.capabilities into BTreeSet<String>.
- capabilities_granted = req.capabilities_requested.iter().filter(|c| connector_caps.contains(c.as_str())).cloned().map(...).collect() — intersection only.
- HandshakeResponse uses the adapter-generated session_id (not fresh SessionId::new()).

Template commits: d18041eb (fcp-e2e/terraform), c9561961 (fcp-e2e/spotify). Each applies ~70 LOC per file. Mechanical; low-risk; can ship as a single batched commit or as 14 connector-scoped commits.

Related context:
- offline_repair_e2e.rs: N/A (no connector handshake; out of scope).
- s3_compliance_e2e.rs: architecturally different — the S3 connector emits a full HandshakeResponse shape and verifies capabilities internally (connector.rs:782). Adapter's serde_json::from_value(response) roundtrip and absent adapter-level invoke verify are consistent with that design. No fix needed here.
- spotify/terraform: already FIXED.
- CapabilityVerifier::verify owned-token fanout (t5453): uniformly applied across all 14 MASKED suites. No additional fanout work required.

Suggested severity: P1/HIGH. Same class of bug as the already-closed br-619oy (terraform) and br-9sztj / br-a6ouw (spotify). Filing as a single roll-up because the fix is mechanical — an IMPLEMENTER can rotate through all 14 with the template in ~4 hours.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:17:51.697069Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:13:53.225300Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T20:13:53.225051Z","close_reason":"Resolved by commits 9fbcbcb1 (cod_1 last 7 harnesses) + 130772a0 (cc_2 first 7 harnesses); all 14 masked-handshake suites now invoke real connector","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2847,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rz5y1","author":"jemanuel","text":"First half (7 of 14 suites) shipped in commit 130772a0 by cc_2. Suites fixed: mailchimp, metabase, monday, n8n, onepassword, postgresql, retool. All 21 tests pass (3 per suite). Template applied from c9561961 / d18041eb. Bundled prerequisite build fix: crates/fcp-sandbox/src/egress.rs:811 canonicalize_host → canonicalize_hostname (Ok(...) pattern since it returns Result not Option). Sibling half (segment, sendgrid, snowflake, todoist, trello, webhook_receiver, zapier) pending — cod_1 assigned.","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:04:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-rzvi1","title":"Add 'status' field to manifest.toml schema (ready/stub/deprecated) for honest connector classification","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:17.242866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:00.031986Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:00.031809Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rzvi1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ve470","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:32.596017Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1960,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rzvi1","author":"jemanuel","text":"FOUNDATION TASK: Enables honest reporting for scaffolded connectors.

Add to manifest.toml [connector] section:
  status = "ready"  # ready | stub | deprecated | experimental

Semantics:
- ready: fully functional, all declared operations work
- stub: operations are declared but return 'not implemented' — useful for planning/documentation
- deprecated: connector is being phased out, prefer alternative
- experimental: operations may work but are not production-tested

Update fcp-manifest crate:
1. Add ConnectorStatus enum with serde deserialization
2. Default to 'ready' if field is absent (backwards compatible)
3. Add to ManifestMetadata struct

Update fwc:
1. fwc list: show status column, highlight non-ready connectors
2. fwc show: report status prominently
3. fwc invoke: warn (not block) if status != ready

Update fcp-host:
1. Include status in discovery/introspect responses
2. Log warning when loading non-ready connectors

Steps:
1. Add ConnectorStatus enum to fcp-manifest
2. Parse from manifest.toml [connector] section
